target: Migrate hard-coded workspace assumptions into profile files and finalize the initial integration-profile system.
invariants: Preserve the tmux-host repo structure under src/tmux_host/ and keep subsystem boundaries intact.
Do not reintroduce Dopemux-specific ownership into host core; Dopemux remains an integration profile.
Keep desktop, mobile, CLI, and tmux-native surfaces consuming the same derived state model where UI/state files are touched.
Do not broaden allowlists or mix unrelated cleanup into these packets.
Workspace shape must come from profile data, not shell branching or hard-coded repo paths in core logic.
Dopemux support remains an integration profile, not a host-core dependency.

commit.message: Migrate workspaces into profile-driven integration system

steps.task: Document the integration-profile model and add example workspace profiles for Dopemux, CRM, and a generic repo.
requirements: Document Dopemux as an integration profile only.
Keep profile examples aligned to the strict schema from Series A.

steps.task: Wire profile loading and normalization so workspace composition is profile-driven rather than hard-coded in runtime logic.
requirements: Do not add profile inheritance or dynamic magic.
Keep workspace composition deterministic and explicit.

steps.task: Add integration tests that prove workspace materialization can be driven by profile examples, including the Dopemux integration profile.
requirements: Keep tests focused on profile-driven workspace behavior.
Do not make the host core depend on Dopemux internals.
